title: Pilot

jokes.text: So you've come to the master for guidance? Is this what you're saying, Grasshopper?
jokes.setup: Michael presents himself as a wise mentor
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately deflates this by pointing out Michael called him in
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor establishing Michael's delusional self-importance. The Grasshopper reference and immediate deflation works well.

jokes.text: Actually, you called me in here. But, yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael's grandiose mentor pose
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact correction with reluctant agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Economy is excellent - maximum deflation with minimum words.

jokes.text: Just wanted to talk to you manager a manager.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound professional on a sales call
jokes.punchline: Stumbles over basic phrase 'manager to manager'
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Michael's nervousness undermining his confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Okay. I'm sorry. My mistake. That was a woman I was talking to. So. She had a very low voice. Probably a smoker. So...
jokes.setup: Michael confidently calling someone 'gentleman and scholar'
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtracking and inappropriate speculation about smoking
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael moment - trying to cover embarrassment makes it worse. The smoking speculation is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: So this is my kingdom, far as the eye can see.
jokes.setup: Michael grandly presenting his domain
jokes.punchline: Visual contrast of modest office space vs. grandiose language
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy relying on disparity between Michael's self-perception and reality.

jokes.text: Pam has been with us, um... for... forever. Right, Pam? Well, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Michael confidently stating Pam's tenure
jokes.punchline: Pam's uncertain, deflating response
jokes.explanation: Nice awkward beat showing Michael's tendency to overstate and Pam's reluctance to go along.

jokes.text: If you think she's cute now, you should have seen her a couple of years ago. Reow, reow.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing Pam professionally
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment with cat sounds
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness. The cat sounds make it extra cringey and memorable.

jokes.text: What?
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Pam's shocked, simple reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word captures the audience's reaction. Excellent structure as the necessary beat after Michael's inappropriateness.

jokes.text: It's called the wastepaper basket!
jokes.setup: Michael explaining special filing system for corporate documents
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just throws them away
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff structure. Michael's anti-corporate attitude delivered with perfect timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Look at that! Oh, look at that face.
jokes.setup: Michael's wastepaper basket reveal
jokes.punchline: Michael delighting in Pam's reaction
jokes.explanation: Michael's pride in getting a reaction shows his need for attention and approval.

jokes.text: People say I am the best boss. They go, 'God, we've never worked in a place like this before. You're hilarious. And you get the best out of us.' Um... I think that pretty much sums it up.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about being loved
jokes.punchline: Obvious delusion given what we've just witnessed
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience knows this is completely false based on what we've seen. Pure Michael delusion.

jokes.text: I'm... I'm boring myself just talking about this.
jokes.setup: Jim describing his mundane job duties
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on how dull his work is
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor. Jim breaking the fourth wall slightly while staying in character. Very relatable and quotable.

jokes.text: Wassuuuup? I still love that after seven years. Whasuuuup?
jokes.setup: Michael using dated pop culture reference
jokes.punchline: His pride in still finding it funny after seven years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - clinging to outdated reference and being proud of it. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: I call her Hillary Rodham Clinton. Right? Not to her face, because, uh... Well, not because I'm scared of her. Because I'm not.
jokes.setup: Michael's tough-guy persona about his boss
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracking and revealing he is scared
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-contradiction. Tries to seem tough but immediately reveals his fear.

jokes.text: Mmm... me no get an agenda.
jokes.setup: Professional phone call with corporate
jokes.punchline: Michael speaking like a caveman
jokes.explanation: Weird Michael quirk that shows his immaturity during important business calls. Memorable but somewhat cheap.

jokes.text: You put it in the garbage can that was a special filing cabinet.
jokes.setup: Michael's earlier wastepaper basket joke
jokes.punchline: Pam throwing it back at him when it causes problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows consequences of Michael's earlier joke. Pam gets a rare moment of getting him back.

jokes.text: Yeah! Uh, that was a joke. That was a joke that, uh, was actually my brother's. And it was... it was supposed to be with bills, and it doesn't work great with faxes.
jokes.setup: Michael caught in his own joke backfiring
jokes.punchline: Desperate backtracking and blaming his brother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael behavior - can't admit fault so creates elaborate excuse. The brother blame is particularly pathetic.

jokes.text: Me no wanna hear that, Jan. Because downsizing is a bitch. It is a real bitch.
jokes.setup: Serious corporate discussion about layoffs
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate caveman speak and language
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate response to serious news. The caveman speak during business crisis is peak Michael.

jokes.text: Not downsizing himself, but is he concerned about downsizing? Ha ha ha ha!
jokes.setup: Jan asking a serious question about competition
jokes.punchline: Michael making awful pun about Josh's weight and laughing alone
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that only Michael finds funny. Shows his inappropriate timing and bad sense of humor.

jokes.text: Hey, you big queen! Oh, that's not... appropriate.
jokes.setup: Michael taking call from Todd Packer
jokes.punchline: Todd's offensive greeting and Michael's weak objection
jokes.explanation: Establishes Todd Packer's awful character and Michael's weak protests against inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Does the carpet match the drapes? Oh, my God. Ohh! That's... horrifying. And horrible. Horrible person.
jokes.setup: Todd asking inappropriate question about Jan
jokes.punchline: Michael's horrified but fascinated reaction
jokes.explanation: Michael's conflicted reaction - horrified but not hanging up. Shows his weakness for inappropriate humor.

jokes.text: As a doctor, you would not tell a patient if they had cancer.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he won't tell employees about downsizing
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards medical analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that's completely wrong but delivered with Michael's usual confidence. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Watch this, this is Moe. Nyuck! Nyuck! Nyuck! Nyuck! Nyuck! Meeeeh! Ha ha! Ah, right here. Three Stooges. High five! Oh, Pam. It's a guy thing, Pam.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to bond with new temp Ryan
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing Three Stooges impression that falls flat
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cringe trying too hard to connect with Ryan. The 'guy thing' dismissal to Pam adds extra inappropriateness.

jokes.text: I'm Hitler! Adolf Hitler...
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his 'comedy' demonstration
jokes.punchline: Does Hitler impression, realizes how inappropriate it is mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Shocking moment that perfectly captures Michael's complete lack of boundaries. The trail-off shows even he knows he's gone too far.

jokes.text: I don't think it would be the worst thing if they let me go. Because then I might... I just, I don't think it's many little girls' dream to be a receptionist.
jokes.setup: Discussion of potential layoffs
jokes.punchline: Pam's honest assessment of her job satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Sweet, sad humor showing Pam's dreams vs reality. Good character depth with gentle humor.

jokes.text: One word, two syllables: demarcation.
jokes.setup: Dwight arguing about desk space boundaries
jokes.punchline: Overly formal word choice for petty office dispute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - taking trivial matter seriously with unnecessarily formal language. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Safety violation. I could fall and pierce... an organ.
jokes.setup: Dwight objecting to Jim's pencils on his desk
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction about potential injury
jokes.explanation: Dwight's paranoid catastrophizing over pencils. The dramatic pause before 'an organ' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I have been recommending downsizing since I first got here. I even brought it up in my interview.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming downsizing doesn't bother him
jokes.punchline: Reveals he suggested firing people in his job interview
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic - so eager to please he'd suggest firing colleagues in his interview. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Sha-ta-ta-ta-ta-ta-ta! Six Million Dollar Man! Steve Austin! Actually, that would be a good salary for me, don't you think? $6 million?
jokes.setup: Michael making Six Million Dollar Man reference
jokes.punchline: Takes it literally as a salary request
jokes.explanation: Michael missing the point of his own reference and making it about money. Mild character moment.

jokes.text: People I respect, heroes of mine, would be... Bob Hope. Um... Abraham Lincoln. Definitely. Bono. Uh, and probably God... would be the fourth one.
jokes.setup: Michael listing his heroes
jokes.punchline: Puts God as fourth choice after Bono
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael priorities - God comes after a rock star. The casual 'probably' makes it even better.

jokes.text: It's really incalcalacable.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound profound about his heroes
jokes.punchline: Mangles the word 'incalculable'
jokes.explanation: Michael trying to sound smart but stumbling over big words. The extra syllables make it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm Assistant Regional Manager. I should know first. Assistant to the Regional Manager.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming important title
jokes.punchline: Jim's correction showing Dwight's inflated sense of importance
jokes.explanation: Iconic running gag establishing Jim/Dwight dynamic. Perfect economy showing their relationship in few words.

jokes.text: You know, you can go mess with Josh's people. But I'm the head of this family, and you ain't gonna be messin' with my chillun'.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound protective of his employees
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate dialect and 'chillun' pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate attempt at folksy authority. The dialect switch is classic Michael obliviousness.

jokes.text: Pam's favorite flavor of yogurt, which is mixed berry. Jim said mixed berries? Oh, well, yeah, he's on to me.
jokes.setup: Jim listing useless work knowledge
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's revealed his attention to Pam
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Jim's crush on Pam through seemingly mundane detail. Good relationship building.

jokes.text: He put my stuff in Jell-O again! That's real professional. Thanks. This is the third time, and it wasn't funny the first two times either, Jim!
jokes.setup: Office prank involving stapler in Jell-O
jokes.punchline: Dwight's outraged reaction and admission this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office prank that became cultural touchstone. Visual gag with perfect character reactions. Third time reference shows pattern.

jokes.text: You have to eat it out of there, because there are starving people in the world, and, which I hate, and it it is a waste of that kind of food.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to resolve Jell-O prank appropriately
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic about world hunger and Jell-O waste
jokes.explanation: Michael's nonsensical attempt to turn prank into moral lesson. The parenthetical about hating starving people is perfect.

jokes.text: Dwight, I'm sorry, because... I have always been your biggest flan.
jokes.setup: Jim's fake apology to Dwight
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fan' with dessert theme continuing Jell-O joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun that extends the Jell-O theme while delivering fake apology. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: You, uh, you should have put him in 'custardy.'
jokes.setup: Building on Jim's flan pun
jokes.punchline: Custody/custard pun
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up pun showing Ryan trying to fit in. Solid wordplay extending the dessert theme.

jokes.text: Pudding. Putting. I'm trying to think what other dessert to do.
jokes.setup: Chain of dessert puns
jokes.punchline: Michael trying to force more puns and failing
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate attempt to join the pun chain but can't make it work. Shows his need to be included.

jokes.text: Do I think I'll be invited to the wedding?
jokes.setup: Pam and Roy's engagement and Michael's relationship with employees
jokes.punchline: Michael's uncertain, hopeful question to camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward beat showing Michael's delusional relationship with his employees. The uncertainty is touching and funny.

jokes.text: I am going to have to let you go first. What? Why? Well, theft. And stealing.
jokes.setup: Michael fake-firing Pam as a prank
jokes.punchline: Redundant charges of 'theft and stealing'
jokes.explanation: Michael's redundancy shows his nervousness about the prank. Sets up the cruel fake firing sequence.

jokes.text: You steal 1,000 Post-It notes at 50 cents apiece, and, you know, you've made a profit... margin.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Pam's supposed theft
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical math and trailing off into business jargon
jokes.explanation: Michael's business logic falls apart mid-sentence. The 'profit margin' doesn't make sense but sounds businessy to him.

jokes.text: You've been 'Xed,' punk! Surprise! It's a joke! We were joking around! See?
jokes.setup: Michael's cruel fake firing prank
jokes.punchline: References wrong show ('X'd' instead of 'Punk'd') and desperate damage control
jokes.explanation: Michael's botched TV reference and panic when prank goes wrong. Classic Michael move of making it worse.

jokes.text: It was a young Guatemalan guy. First job in the country, barely spoke English. Came to me and said, 'Mr. Scott, would you be the godfather of my child?' Wow. Wow. Wow. Didn't work out in the end. We had to let him go. He sucked.
jokes.setup: Heartwarming story about immigrant employee asking Michael to be godfather
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation they fired him because 'he sucked'
jokes.explanation: Masterful setup/payoff structure. The heartwarming story builds emotion perfectly before the devastating punchline. Peak Michael delusion and callousness.

jokes.text: Listen, have a nice weekend. Yeah, definitely. You too. Enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam's obvious attraction and her engagement to Roy
jokes.punchline: Painfully polite interaction hiding deeper feelings
jokes.explanation: Subtle character comedy showing unspoken attraction through overly formal politeness. Builds romantic tension.

tmdb_overview: A documentary crew arrives at the offices of Dunder Mifflin to observe the employees and learn about modern management. Manager Michael Scott tries to paint a happy picture, while sales rep Jim fights with his nemesis Dwight and flirts with receptionist Pam.
